message:
test(e2e): Add accessibility test improvements and sync beads

- Expand accessibility E2E test coverage
- Sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -589,7 +589,7 @@
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.3","title":"Help overlay: render from ActionRegistry (no hardcoded shortcuts)","description":"## Goal
Refactor the web help overlay so it *always* reflects current shortcuts.

## Requirements
- Render shortcuts from ActionRegistry (not hardcoded arrays).
- Organize into categories matching the Depth Layers philosophy:
  - Layer 0: sacred surface
  - Layer 1: quick overlays
  - Layer 2+: advanced (analysis menu, simulations, command palette)
- Support “contextual” actions (only show when relevant).

## Acceptance criteria
- Help overlay shows correct shortcuts immediately after keybinding changes.
- No duplicate/conflicting entries.
- UX: clear labeling of scope/context; consistent typography.

## Related
Follow-up/regression hardening for `phage_explorer-m31q` (dynamic hotkey reference).

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.4","title":"Hotkey conflict detection (registry-based)","description":"## Goal
Prevent accidental hotkey conflicts as the app grows.

## Background
A hotkey conflict detector existed in earlier polish work (see `phage_explorer-vss3` / `phage_explorer-ejjg`, closed). The current need is to ensure conflict detection is **powered by the ActionRegistry** so the registry remains the single source of truth.

## Approach
- Add a validator that:
  - scans ActionRegistry shortcuts
  - flags duplicates for the same scope/context
  - optionally flags conflicts with browser-reserved combos
- Run validator at startup in dev/test (not necessarily prod).

## Acceptance criteria
- A test fails if a conflicting shortcut is introduced.
- The validation output clearly points to the conflicting ActionIds.
- The validator checks the registry (not scattered ad-hoc hotkey lists).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T01:49:34.939752457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T02:58:03.632110856Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T02:58:03.632043962Z","close_reason":"Registry-based hotkey conflict validator + test coverage (keyboard/validateConflicts.ts + keyboard/validateConflicts.test.ts)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotkeys","reliability","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.4","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T03:01:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.4","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T03:01:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.5","title":"Regression tests: help/shortcuts cannot drift","description":"## Goal
Add automated coverage that protects the help/shortcut system from regression.

## Suggested tests
- Unit: ActionRegistry uniqueness + stable formatting.
- Component/integration: Help overlay renders registry categories.
- (Optional) e2e: pressing '?' shows expected key groups.

## Acceptance criteria
- A keybinding change requires updating ActionRegistry (test enforces).
- Help overlay cannot silently become stale.

-{"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.6","title":"Optional: derive README keyboard tables from registry","description":"## Goal
Reduce doc drift by deriving README “Keyboard Controls” / keybinding tables from the ActionRegistry.

## Notes
- Only do this if it can be implemented cleanly without a complex build step.
- A lightweight approach: a script that prints a markdown snippet for manual paste.

## Acceptance criteria
- Documentation updates become low-friction and accurate.
- No new runtime dependency introduced.

Run with 'bun scripts/generate-keyboard-tables.ts'. Output can be copy-pasted into README.md.","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:03:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7","title":"Command palette + menus: render actions/shortcuts from ActionRegistry","description":"## Problem
Even if the Help overlay is correct, users still experience drift if:
- the command palette shows outdated shortcuts
- menus/panels show inconsistent labels

## Goal
Use the ActionRegistry as the source of truth for action metadata and shortcut display across:
- command palette
- analysis menu / overlay open menus (where applicable)
- footer hints / subtle shortcut hints

## Approach
- Ensure command palette items reference an `ActionId`.
- Display shortcuts via ActionRegistry formatting utilities.
- Keep the command palette performant (no importing overlay bundles to list actions).

## Acceptance criteria
- Command palette always shows correct shortcuts.
- Shortcut display is consistent (formatting, modifiers, case).
- Adding a new action requires updating ActionRegistry, not multiple UI lists.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7.1","title":"AnalysisSidebar: derive tool labels + shortcut badges from ActionRegistry","description":"## Problem
The Analysis sidebar displays tool entries with shortcut badges, but those shortcut badges can drift from actual behavior.

This creates immediate user distrust:
- users learn a shortcut from the sidebar
- press it
- nothing happens (or a different action happens)

### Evidence / where to look
- `packages/web/src/components/AnalysisSidebar.tsx` hardcodes shortcut badges (previously observed e.g. around ~L48 and ~L79).
- `packages/web/src/keyboard/actionRegistry.ts` defines canonical shortcuts (previously observed e.g. Hilbert and Packaging Pressure around ~L294).

Example drift (as previously observed):
- Sidebar shows `H` for Hilbert Curve, but registry expects `Shift+H`
- Sidebar shows `v` for Packaging Pressure, but registry expects `Shift+V`

## Steps to Reproduce
1) Open the web app (desktop viewport).
2) Locate the Analysis sidebar tool list.
3) Find a tool with a visible shortcut badge (e.g., “Hilbert Curve” or “Packaging Pressure”).
4) Press the shortcut exactly as shown in the badge (e.g., `H` or `v`).
5) Observe the tool/overlay does not open (or the wrong tool opens).
6) Compare against the ActionRegistry shortcut (e.g., `Shift+H` / `Shift+V`) which *does* open the correct tool.

## Root cause
The sidebar is a user-facing shortcut documentation surface but it is not driven by the ActionRegistry (single source of truth).

## Goal
Make the Analysis sidebar a *trusted* discoverability surface by ensuring:
- labels and shortcut badges are derived from ActionRegistry
- no hardcoded key strings exist in the sidebar

## Proposed approach
- Represent each sidebar entry by an `ActionId` (the same IDs used by `useHotkey`).
- Render shortcut badges using ActionRegistry formatting.
- If a tool has multiple shortcuts, display the platform-appropriate primary shortcut.

## Testing plan (must)
### Unit (Bun)
- Add a unit test that fails if:
  - a sidebar entry references an invalid `ActionId`
  - the displayed shortcut badge does not match ActionRegistry formatting

Prefer a pure “sidebar config” module exporting `ActionId[]` or entries so tests avoid brittle DOM rendering.

### E2E (Playwright)
- Add an assertion that at least one sidebar entry:
  - displays the expected shortcut
  - opens the expected overlay when the shortcut is pressed

### Logging requirements
- When a mismatch is detected, logs must print:
  - ActionId
  - expected shortcut(s)
  - rendered badge

## Acceptance criteria
- No shortcut strings are hardcoded in `packages/web/src/components/AnalysisSidebar.tsx`.
- Every sidebar entry corresponds to an ActionRegistry `ActionId`.
- Shortcut badges always match actual hotkey behavior.
- Unit + e2e coverage prevents drift from reappearing.

## Related
QA umbrella: `phage_explorer-s4qx.8.6` (unit) and `phage_explorer-s4qx.8.3` (e2e).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:44:26.453603696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:52:27.078447825Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:52:27.078447825Z","close_reason":"Implemented ActionRegistry-driven AnalysisSidebar + drift-proof tests","compaction_level":0,"labels":["hotkeys","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:44:26.455537180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7.2","title":"Mobile ActionDrawer: fix 'Selection' item opening Packaging Pressure overlay","description":"## Problem
On mobile, the ActionDrawer had an item label/action mismatch:
- the drawer displayed an item labeled “Selection”
- tapping it opened the **Packaging Pressure** overlay

User impact:
- UI label did not match the action performed
- felt random/untrustworthy

### Evidence / where to look
- `packages/web/src/components/controls/ActionDrawer.tsx` maps drawer items to actions (previously observed around ~L163).

## Steps to Reproduce (historical)
1) Use a mobile viewport (or a real mobile device).
2) Open the ActionDrawer.
3) Tap the list item labeled “Selection”.
4) Observe that the Packaging Pressure tool/overlay opens.

## Root cause
ActionDrawer’s item → action mapping is hand-wired and labels can drift from the underlying overlay/tool.

## Resolution
This issue was closed after correcting the label to match the actual action (Packaging Pressure).

## Follow-up (important)
Even if the label is now correct, this category of drift can reappear.

Track the durable fix (registry-driven drawer + tests) separately:
- create/maintain a task under `phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7` to derive drawer items from ActionRegistry and add regression coverage (unit + e2e).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:44:46.620407825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:33:07.753063338Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:11:30.155027968Z","close_reason":"Fixed label from 'Selection' to 'Packaging Pressure' in ActionDrawer.tsx line 163","compaction_level":0,"labels":["hotkeys","mobile","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7.2","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:44:46.622306638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
